description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, speckled stone material that forms the raised sections. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with clean, straight edges and a uniform thickness. The stone surface displays a natural, marbled texture with subtle color variations, suggesting it is made from granite or a similar hard stone. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the functional, utilitarian design. This type of block is commonly used as a tactile paving or barrier in urban environments to guide or restrict pedestrian movement.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top, featuring a marbled stone texture and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, smooth front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regular spacing of the grooves across the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or pedestrian crossing zone
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with pavement or adjacent blocks
